title: PAMSPEC Evidence Record
description: One machine-readable record about a single claim made against a PAMSPEC requirement. Records live in append-only evidence chains (JSONL files). A record whose claim is later shown to be wrong is NEVER deleted; it is marked `retracted` and a new record with the correcting result is added with `supersedes_claim` pointing back at the retracted record's `record_id`. This is the R6 evidence model; it is not a PAMSPEC normative wire type.

record_id.description: Stable, unique identifier for this record. Referenced by `supersedes_claim` in later records. Convention: `<sprint>.<subject>.<kind>`, e.g. `v08.scenario7.probe`, `v08.1.scenario7.controlled_experiment`.

recorded_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this record was authored (not when the test ran, unless they are the same event).

test_kind.description: probe = 'what does this external framework currently do?'; conformance = 'does this implementation satisfy PAMSPEC's normative requirement?'; regression = 'did previously observed behavior change?'; controlled_experiment = 'is the observed signal distinguishable from an experimental artifact?'

requirement_id.description: Dotted identifier of the PAMSPEC requirement or property under evaluation, e.g. `PAMSPEC.scope.immutable_across_update`, `PAMSPEC.authoritative_derived.vector_refresh_on_update`. Stable across records so a claim's history can be reassembled by `requirement_id`.

framework.description: Name of the framework under test, e.g. `mem0ai`, `letta`, `langgraph`. Null when the record is about the PAMSPEC reference implementation or the spec itself (test_kind=conformance against reference-python).

framework_version.description: Exact version string of the framework under test. Null when framework is null.

classification.description: Observed outcome for the requirement against the framework/implementation. native = provided directly. emulated = adapter-side compensation would be needed. gap = the requirement cannot be met with the current public API. questionable = evidence suggests the requirement itself may be artificial. not_testable = the scenario cannot be probed with the current public API without modifying the framework.

sub_requirements.description: Zero or more finer-grained sub-requirement classifications. Each entry classifies one aspect of the top-level requirement independently, so a top-level `emulated` can carry, for example, `{native, native, not_testable, emulated}` internally.

evidence_source.description: How the evidence was obtained. `public_api` = only public interfaces of the framework/implementation. `backend_inspection` = read-only inspection of an underlying store (e.g. Chroma collection). `adapter` = observed through a PAMSPEC adapter, not directly. `source_review` = derived from reading the framework's source, not from execution. Multiple sources may apply.

claim_status.description: Lifecycle of the claim this record makes. `confirmed` = the record's stated classification is supported by the evidence. `inconclusive` = the experiment ran but cannot distinguish among alternative explanations. `retracted` = a later record has shown this record's claim to be wrong; retracted records MUST remain in the evidence chain and a superseding record MUST exist. `superseded` = a later record replaced this one without contradicting it (e.g. re-run with better methodology and matching result). `not_testable` = the claim cannot be evaluated with the current framework surface.

supersedes_claim.description: The `record_id` of the record this record supersedes (with or without contradiction). MUST be present when a prior record for the same `requirement_id` exists. Set to null only for the first record on a given requirement.

control_design.description: Structured description of the experimental controls used. REQUIRED when `test_kind` is `controlled_experiment`. Recommended for `probe` when a control set was used. Null otherwise.

control_count.description: Number of decoy/distractor entries populated to distinguish the target signal from ordinary nearest-neighbor behavior.

distinctive_phrases.description: Whether target-before and target-after used maximally unrelated distinctive text so a similarity change is detectable.

direct_signal_captured.description: Whether the primary signal (e.g. stored embedding bytes) was captured directly rather than inferred from downstream behavior.

alternative_explanations_ruled_out.description: Explicit list of alternative explanations that the design rules out (and how).

environment_manifest.description: Reference to the environment manifest under which the evidence was produced. The referenced file is not required to be inline; the (path, sha256) pair is enough to make the environment traceable. Null only when explicitly not applicable.

reference.description: Repo-relative path to the environment-manifest.json (or equivalent).

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the referenced file at the recorded commit.

results_hash.description: SHA-256 of the raw machine-readable evidence file (e.g. the JSONL emitted by the test scenario) this record summarizes. Null only when explicitly not applicable (e.g. this record IS the raw evidence).

evidence_commit.description: Git commit SHA where the raw evidence file lives. MUST be an immutable commit that is expected never to move (outreach permalinks and scientific reproducibility depend on this).

limitations.description: Explicit honest list of what this evidence does NOT establish. If the experiment cannot distinguish two alternative explanations, say so here.

observed_evidence.description: Concrete key values observed (e.g. rank_before, rank_after, l2_delta). Not schema-validated in detail because it is per-scenario. This is the durable record of 'what was seen', not 'what was concluded'.

conclusion_narrative.description: One paragraph, plain-English, explaining what the record's classification and claim_status mean in context. NOT a replacement for `observed_evidence`.
